steps.title: Find the decisive signal
steps.content: Robots have "unnatural aspects" in their appearance that hinder interaction, and the features should help counteract these aspects.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
The blank needs a verb meaning "to lessen / reduce / soften" — the features should COUNTERACT the unnatural-looking aspects of the robot. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — ameliorate: improve or make less severe; bipedal locomotion would reduce the negative effect of unnatural features. ✓
  • Choice B — coalesce: combine or merge; you can't merge an effect, the word doesn't fit the syntax. ✗
  • Choice C — augment: increase or enhance; this would INCREASE the unnatural-looking effect, the opposite of what designers want. ✗
  • Choice D — substantiate: support with evidence; we are not proving anything about the effect. ✗
That is the correct answer.
